title: GHRELIN
description: Ghrelin is a 28-amino acid gastric peptide hormone that serves as the endogenous ligand for the growth hormone secretagogue receptor (GHSR). It plays a critical role in neuroendocrine regulation by stimulating growth hormone release, Increasing appetite (orexigenic effect), and coordinating energy metabolism between the gut and the brain.

research.title: Molecular Mechanisms and Health Benefits of Ghrelin: A Narrative Review
research.summary: This review details how ghrelin activates the GHSR to promote appetite, pleasure-reward eating, and various metabolic functions throughout the body.

research.title: Structure and physiological actions of ghrelin
research.summary: This research explains the unique n-octanoylation modification of ghrelin required for its biological activity and its production in the stomach and hypothalamus.

research.title: Ghrelin: Integrative Neuroendocrine Peptide in Health and Disease
research.summary: Researchers identified ghrelin as a mediator of growth hormone release that coordinates energy needs with growth processes and body weight regulation.

research.title: Ghrelin: Structure and Function
research.summary: A foundational study using reverse pharmacology to identify ghrelin as the natural ligand for the growth hormone secretagogue receptor in rat and human tissues.

sentiment.summary: Users generally value Ghrelin mimetics for muscle growth and recovery, but frequently report significant challenges with intense hunger and potential blood sugar disruption.

effects.label: Appetite Induction:
effects.description: Highly effective at triggering hunger, making it useful for those struggling with wasting syndromes or poor caloric intake.

effects.label: Muscle Recovery:
effects.description: Users report improved recovery times and better sleep quality when using ghrelin-mimicking peptides.

effects.label: Anabolic Synergy:
effects.description: Often cited as most effective when combined with other compounds to maximize growth hormone pulses.

dosage.label: Frequency:
dosage.description: Commonly administered via peptide mimetics (like Ipamorelin or GHRPs) 1-3 times daily.

dosage.label: Timing:
dosage.description: Most effective when taken on an empty stomach to avoid interference from blood glucose and insulin levels.

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Users often recommend cycling the compound to prevent receptor desensitization.

sideEffects.label: Intense Hunger:
sideEffects.description: The most common side effect, which can lead to unwanted fat gain if diet is not strictly controlled.

sideEffects.label: Water Retention:
sideEffects.description: Users frequently report 'moon face' or edema due to sodium and water storage.

sideEffects.label: Lethargy:
sideEffects.description: High pulses of growth hormone can lead to daytime sleepiness or a 'heavy' feeling.

sideEffects.label: Insulin Resistance:
sideEffects.description: Long-term use or high doses may negatively impact glycemic control.

availability.label: Research Chemicals:
availability.description: Primarily sourced as research-grade peptides through specialized online vendors.

availability.label: Clinical Use:
availability.description: Approved pharmaceutical versions (like Anamorelin) are regulated and typically indicated for cancer cachexia.
